Hosteen Frey is a knight of House Frey, the sixth son of Lord Walder Frey, the first one born of his third wife, Amarei Crakehall. He is married to Bellena Hawick.[3]

Appearance and Character[ ]

See also: Images of Hosteen Frey

Hosteen is a husky, powerful man with a square face and strong jaw.[4][5] He is brawny, on account of his Crakehall ancestry, with a jut of jaw and arms thick with muscle.[6] He speaks bluntly[4] and has a booming voice.[7]

Hosteen is a seasoned soldier and the fiercest fighter of Walder's brood.[6] He favors his mother's family and is considered the strongest of all the Freys.[4][8] The knight carries a longsword.[7]

Stannis Baratheon judges Hosteen to be dim and stupid,[9] and Theon Greyjoy compares him to a bull.[6]

History[ ]

Ser Hosteen participated in the tourney at Lannisport held to celebrate King Robert I Baratheon's victory over Greyjoy's Rebellion. He was defeated by the eventual champion, Ser Jorah Mormont.[10]

Hosteen rode in the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day in 298 AC, but he was unhorsed by Ser Loras Tyrell.[11]

Recent Events[ ]

A Game of Thrones[ ]

Hosteen attends the Hand's tourney in King's Landing with six of his kin—Ser Jared, Ser Danwell, Ser Emmon, Ser Theo, Ser Perwyn, and Martyn Rivers.[12]

Hosteen is at the Twins when Catelyn Stark informs her son, Robb, of the alliance negotiated with Walder Frey, Lord of the Crossing.[11]

A Clash of Kings[ ]

Hosteen is a captive of Lord Tywin Lannister, together with his brothers Ser Jared and Ser Danwell Frey and his half-brother Ronel Rivers.[3] Tywin holds captives from the battle on the Green Fork at the Tower of Dread in Harrenhal.[13] Arya Stark observes the four Freys exercising in the Flowstone Yard of Harrenhal and then being ransomed by their kin.[13]

Hosteen is a member of the Frey contingent commanded by Ser Aenys Frey. When Lord Roose Bolton holds a war council after the Battle of the Blackwater, Hosteen insists that the war is lost and that Robb, who is now King in the North, must yield.[4]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Hosteen Frey, by Cloudninja9 ©

Ser Hosteen is among the Freys who meet Ser Jaime Lannister when the maimed Kingslayer arrives at Harrenhal with the Brave Companions.[14]

Hosteen is introduced to Catelyn Stark at the Twins on the eve of Lord Edmure Tully's wedding to Roslin Frey.[5] Lothar Frey says something amusing to Hosteen during the wedding feast. Hosteen and Aenys pound their cups on the table to bring attention to Lord Walder when their father wants the bedding custom to begin.[15] During the ensuing massacre which becomes known as the Red Wedding, Hosteen cuts down Lucas Blackwood.[15]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Aenys and Hosteen command the two thousand Frey swords who are sent with Lord Bolton to retake the north.[16]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

Hosteen and Aeny accompany Roose past Moat Cailin[6] to Barrowton.[17] They become distressed when three of their kin—Ser Jared Frey, Rhaegar, and Symond—disappear after returning the bones of Ser Wendel Manderly to Lord Wyman Manderly at White Harbor.[17][18]

Hosteen and Aenys are present at the wedding of Ramsay Bolton to "Arya Stark", who is actually Jeyne Poole, at Winterfell. Lord Manderly personally serves slices of pork pie to Aenys and Hosteen during the celebration.[19] In the worsening cold, Hosteen loses an ear to frostbite.[18]

When several men-at-arms are murdered, Hosteen at first suspects Theon Greyjoy but later suspects Wyman of ordering the deaths.[18] When Little Walder Frey is found dead at the First Keep, Hosteen brings his body into the Great Hall of Winterfell. Hosteen accuses Lord Manderly, who denies the charge while insulting House Frey. This enrages Hosteen, who cuts Wyman's neck and would have killed him if not for the intervention of Wyman's men. When one lunges at Hosteen with a dagger, the Frey dodges and cuts off his attacker's arm. After several men are killed and wounded, Dreadfort spearmen prevent the situation from escalating further. Roose Bolton sends Hosteen and Aenys in the vanguard to attack Stannis Baratheon's army.[7]

The Winds of Winter[ ]

 Warning
This information has thus far been released in a sample chapter for The Winds of Winter, and might therefore not be in finalized form. Keep in mind that the content as described below is still subject to change.

The Frey vanguard falls into a pit trap set by Mors Umber. Hosteen loses a horse and Aenys is killed, placing Hosteen, whom Stannis refers to as "Ser Stupid", in command of the Frey force sent to battle Stannis.[9]

Quotes by Hosteen[ ]

Hosteen: Someone must have the courage to say it. The war is lost. King Robb must be made to see that.

Roose: His Grace has defeated the Lannisters every time he has faced them in battle.

Hosteen: He has lost the north. He has lost Winterfell! His brothers are dead ...[4]

—Hosteen and Roose Bolton

Wyman: White Harbor does not fear to ride with you, Ser Hosteen. Lead us out, and my knights will ride behind you.
Hosteen: Close enough to drive a lance through my back, aye. Where are my kin, Manderly? Tell me that.[18]

—Wyman Manderly and Hosteen

Roose: Ser Hosteen, assemble your knights and men-at-arms by the main gates. As you are so eager for battle, you shall strike our first blow. Lord Wyman, gather your White Harbor men by the east gate. They shall go forth as well.
Hosteen: As my lord commands. But after I deliver you the head of Stannis Baratheon, I mean to finish hacking off Lord Lard's.[7]

—Roose Bolton and Hosteen

Quotes about Hosteen[ ]

Why did I need to ride all that way to see Hosteen knocked off his horse by that Tyrell whelp? I ask you. The boy's half his age, Ser Daisy they call him, something like that.[11]

—Walder Frey to Catelyn Stark

Hosteen and Aenys. He remembered them from before he knew his name. Hosteen was a bull, slow to anger but implacable once roused, and by repute the fiercest fighter of Lord Walder's get. Aenys was older, crueler, and more clever—a commander, not a swordsman. Both were seasoned soldiers.[6]

—thoughts of Theon Greyjoy

They are strangers here as well, he thought, watching Ser Aenys Frey and his half-brother Ser Hosteen. Born and bred in the riverlands, the Freys had never seen a snow like this.[20]

—thoughts of Theon Greyjoy

Theon: Aenys broke his neck, I heard, but Ser Hosteen only lost a horse, more's the pity. He will be angry now.
Stannis: Angry foes do not concern me. Anger makes men stupid, and Hosteen Frey was stupid to begin with, if half of what I have heard of him is true. Let him come.[9]

—Theon Greyjoy and Stannis Baratheon
